raw_consumer_statement:
This dispute concerns a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX purchase and related design/measurement services beginning in XX/XX/XXXX. The cabinetry delivered did not conform to the approved specifications, including delivery of a different cabinet finish ( XXXX XXXX  instead of the approved XXXX ). During installation in XX/XX/XXXX, the kitchen layout designed and measured through XXXX XXXX was discovered to be functionally defective because it failed to account for the basement door swing, making the approved XXXX sink base unusable. XXXX XXXX acknowledged the issue and attempted corrective action. 

A replacement sink base cabinet was later sent, but it was also incorrect because it was the wrong depth and could not properly align with the cabinet run. The kitchen project remains incomplete and nonfunctional despite repeated attempts to resolve the matter directly with the merchant, including permitting replacement attempts and requesting resolution options. 

Citibank denied my billing dispute based in part on conclusions that the merchandise remained in my possession, that it should have been inspected before purchase, and that it was not returned. I disagree with these conclusions because this transaction involved custom cabinetry and related services that could not reasonably be fully inspected until delivery and installation. The defects were identified immediately and promptly reported to the merchant. I also attempted to pursue return and resolution options directly with XXXX XXXX, but adequate resolution was refused. 

This dispute involves materially nonconforming goods, defective replacement merchandise, and faulty design/measurement services rather than ordinary dissatisfaction with a completed purchase. Additionally, another card issuer reviewing related charges arising from the same transaction and project determined that the dispute had sufficient merit to issue a favorable resolution.
